channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Code Smell 320 - Vanity Coverage
item.description: Brushing Over Real Problems TL;DR: You write tests that touch every line but verify nothing, creating false confidence in a broken system. Problems 😔 False confidence Hidden production defects...

item.title: The 45-tool problem: why Juejin 必备 roundups have stopped helping me pick AI tools
item.description: I went down a rabbit hole this morning reading four late-2025 Juejin AI tool roundups back to back, and the thing that finally crystallized for me is that the Juejin "必备" list has quietly inflated...

item.title: Miasma Malware Targets npm Packages and GitHub Actions in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the supply chain attack linked to the Mini Shai-Hulud, Miasma, and Hades malware family that has compromised a new set of npm...

item.title: One Million Passports Leaked Online
item.description: A database of almost a million passports from around the world was leaked online. Note what happened. A high-value credential—a passport—was used in an ancillary low-value authentication system:...

item.title: Building a Multimodal AI Pipeline: Text Image Text Across Three Providers
item.description: Three providers, three modalities, under 55 lines of Python — and a PNG file on disk at the end. Claude writes a sunset description, an image generation model paints it, and Qwen Vision analyzes...

item.title: Building Structured Inter-Agent Communication: A Practical Guide
item.description: Every multi-agent tutorial shows "Agent A talks to Agent B." None show how to keep that conversation reliable at scale. The Problem with String-Based Agent Chat # What most frameworks do: result =...

item.title: You shipped an MCP server. Nobody found it. Here's the fix.
item.description: The problem isn't quality. Most OSS tools die because the author posted once, got silence, and never had time to keep showing up. Distribution is a recurring job — directory submissions, social...

item.title: Three Years in Four Weeks
item.description: In late February 2026, Perplexity AI quietly published a blog post with a claim that should have set off alarms in every corporate office from London to Los Angeles. The company's new product,...

item.title: It’s the last day of Prime Day — here are over 130 great deals to choose from
item.description: We’ve arrived at the final day of Prime Day, which at this point should probably be called “Prime Week.” We’ve found discounts on all manner of gadgets, including TVs, smart home tech, chargers,...

item.title: How to Choose the Right Keywords for Different Geographic Markets
item.description: When you're doing keyword research for a new project, the typical workflow involves brainstorming terms, checking search volume, and maybe looking at difficulty scores. But if you're targeting...

item.title: Knowledge-and-Memory-Management v0.0.2: Knowledge Collection & Memory Management
item.description: Knowledge-and-Memory-Management v0.0.2 is a clean release that introduces structured knowledge collection from web, video, and article sources, alongside memory management enhancements. All...

item.title: Real-Time Network Telemetry for AI: Building an Asynchronous NetFlow/sFlow Ingestion Pipeline in Python
item.description: As AI architectures transition from static data processing to real-time agentic monitoring, the data engineering pipelines feeding them must adapt. If you are building an AI-driven anomaly...

item.title: What Is Loopcraft? From Prompt Engineering to Agent Loop System Design
item.description: 🙋‍ I’m Luhui Dev, a developer who has been breaking down Agent engineering and exploring how AI can be applied in education. I focus on Agent Harness, LLM application engineering, AI for Math, and...

item.title: How Qatar Became FIFA’s Technology Test Lab
item.description: Qatar has become the place where FIFA experiments with the next generation of football technology. The results are already visible across this year’s World Cup.

item.title: Microsoft Warns of Photo ZIP Phishing Campaign Targeting Hotels with Node.js Implant
item.description: An active phishing campaign has been targeting hotel and other hospitality organizations across Europe and Asia since April 2026, using photo-themed ZIP files to drop a Node.js implant and dig...

item.title: Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are trying to figure out why.
item.description: It’s been hot in London this week. Really hot. A dangerous heat wave has hit Western Europe. Yesterday, the UK recorded its highest ever June temperature at 36.1 °C (about 97 °F). But as the...

item.title: Russia Used Cellebrite on Jailed Activist's iPhone Months After Sales Cutoff
item.description: Russian authorities used Cellebrite's UFED forensic tools to break into the iPhone of detained opposition activist Andrey Pivovarov in June 2021, three months after Cellebrite said it would stop...

item.title: Trump Mobile will take your $499 right now
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We still don't have the phones we preordered yet, but this week the T1 hit open sale, no deposit required. Trump Mobile's...

item.title: Google Details Turla's New STOCKSTAY Backdoor Used in Ukraine Espionage Attacks
item.description: The Russian state-sponsored threat actor known as Turla has been attributed to a previously undocumented .NET backdoor called STOCKSTAY that has been deployed against government and military...

item.title: Anthropic Thinks Its Own Success Is Key to Making AI Safe
item.description: Anthropic's critics argue it's rapidly accumulating power. The company says that's what responsible AI development looks like.

item.title: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command
The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
item.description: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.

item.title: Why Amazon Dropped Its OpenAI Movie, Data Center Workers Fight Back, and Meta Leaks Employee Data
item.description: The decision by Amazon-owned MGM Studios to drop the OpenAI movie is just part of AI and film industries becoming increasingly intertwined. On Uncanny Valley, we look at where this is all headed.

item.title: This year’s Prime Day deals on Apple products are the best I’ve seen
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day is now in its third day, and whether you’re looking for a new pair of wireless earbuds or a smartwatch, there’s a good chance you’ll find a discount. The Apple Watch Series 11...

item.title: Android 17’s new foldable gaming mode could make flippy phones more fun
item.description: Android 17 is getting a dedicated gaming mode for foldables that will put a virtual gamepad with touch controls on half of your screen to theoretically make it easier to play games. With foldable...

item.title: YouTube updates Shorts to make it even more like TikTok
item.description: YouTube is adding even more TikTok-like features to Shorts, including a new "clear screen" mode that removes the icons and text from the video you're watching. In a blog post on Thursday, YouTube...

item.title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
item.description: The Trump administration, apprehensive of potential security issues, has reportedly asked OpenAI to stagger the release of its next big-ticket model, GPT-5.6. The Information reported that OpenAI...

item.title: Framework has good news and bad news
item.description: Thanks to the component crisis, it's a bad time to want a new computer. But if you are waiting on a preorder for the Framework Laptop 13 Pro - which Framework's CEO has called the "MacBook Pro for...

item.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build ‘digital worlds’ that stress-test AI agents
item.description: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, is experiencing nearly insatiable demand, its investor says.

item.title: Score a discounted Xbox console before the prices jump
item.description: Microsoft announced today that the price of all Xbox models will rise in August, the second time in less than a year as memory prices continue to wreak havoc on every industry from cars to...
